content.claim: Hungary's education minister (2026) and supporting Hungarian media coverage assert that measured performance differences between selective and comprehensive tracks are primarily explained by family background/selection effects rather than added institutional value, framing the selective system as serving elite self-perpetuation rather than educational improvement.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: This claim is presented by a sitting partisan political actor (minister) with reform incentive, not independent consensus
Sources report the claim is 'broadly consistent with academic segregation literature' but the specific value-added question is not independently adjudicated in sources retrieved
'Competence-measurement data' referenced is not fully detailed; exact methodology and dataset are not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
